meta.project: Maruti India IMS Safety Intelligence Command Center
meta.dataClassification: Synthetic demo data only - no Maruti production data

scenario.name: Near-miss predicted and documented across multi-plant IMS workflow
script: Plant video feed is connected. IMS starts with baseline safety monitoring across material movement zones.
Computer vision flags a human-vehicle proximity breach near a marked aisle and detects PPE confidence drop.
Near-Miss Prediction Agent correlates proximity, aisle congestion, shift timing and historical events.
Incident Documentation Agent drafts an audit-ready near-miss report with timestamped evidence and redaction status.
Compliance Agent maps the event to BNVSAP/Bharat NCAP readiness, AIS traceability and DPDP controls.
Cross-site Tracker compares Gurgaon, Manesar and Gujarat risk patterns and assigns CAPA owners.
IMS Supervisor Agent publishes the evidence pack and prepares the safety-engineering review queue.

plants.name: Gurgaon
plants.primaryRisk: Forklift / pedestrian proximity near trim line material bay

plants.name: Gujarat expansion
plants.primaryRisk: New-zone SOP adherence and contractor onboarding

plants.name: Supplier yard
plants.primaryRisk: Vehicle queueing and document check delays

events.summary: Worker movement path intersects a powered material carrier lane within the next 18 seconds.
Helmet/vest visibility score drops below site threshold because of occlusion and crowding.
Stationary material pallet is causing excess foot traffic deviation near marked boundary.

riskInsights.title: Top driver
riskInsights.body: Aisle density rose 27% above the 14-day baseline during the 09:00 shift handover window.

riskInsights.title: Predictive trigger
riskInsights.body: Near-miss probability crossed the 70% intervention threshold because proximity, occlusion and dwell time rose together.

riskInsights.title: Recommended prevention
riskInsights.body: Trigger voice/andon alert, route material carrier to alternate lane, and assign floor marshal for the next 20 minutes.

incident.title: Predicted near miss: powered vehicle and pedestrian proximity
incident.redaction: Faces blurred, worker identifiers tokenized, access logged
incident.evidence: Video frame sequence, model rationale, proximity score, zone map, CAPA actions
incident.status: Draft ready for EHS supervisor approval

timeline.label: Camera feed ingested
timeline.detail: IMS-CV-09 frame stream linked to material aisle MNS-LG-04.

timeline.label: Risk detected
timeline.detail: Human-vehicle distance estimate crosses safety threshold in active traffic zone.

timeline.label: Automated alert
timeline.detail: Andon + floor marshal recommendation issued before contact event occurs.

timeline.label: Evidence packet
timeline.detail: Video clip, risk score, zone metadata and DPDP redaction status attached.

timeline.label: CAPA created
timeline.detail: Aisle separation reinforcement and shift handover crowding review assigned.

capa.title: Update material carrier routing during shift handover
Contractor refresher on aisle crossing protocol
Review camera occlusion and PPE visibility score

complianceMatrix.whyItMatters: Vehicle safety expectations are rising from compliance to measurable safety performance and traceability.
complianceMatrix.imsControl: Safety intelligence links plant incidents, process defects, containment actions and management review evidence.
complianceMatrix.evidence: Near-miss pack, CAPA closure, management review notes, trend dashboard

complianceMatrix.whyItMatters: AIS obligations require disciplined documentation of process controls, deviations and corrective actions across manufacturing quality and safety workflows.
complianceMatrix.imsControl: Event-to-standard mapping, process audit checklist, corrective action lineage and version-controlled SOP library.
complianceMatrix.evidence: AIS control checklist, SOP retrieval log, action owner history

complianceMatrix.whyItMatters: Incident reporting often captures images, names, IDs, shift details and personal data; access, purpose limitation and deletion need controls.
complianceMatrix.imsControl: Automated redaction, role-based access, purpose tags, retention windows and exportable processing logs.
complianceMatrix.evidence: Redaction hash, access log, consent/purpose tag, retention clock

complianceMatrix.whyItMatters: Local safety procedures must be applied consistently across Gurgaon, Manesar and Gujarat expansion zones.
complianceMatrix.imsControl: SOP Copilot, shift handover alerts, inspection checklist and CAPA follow-up workflow.
complianceMatrix.evidence: SOP answer trace, checklist pass/fail, supervisor sign-off

complianceMatrix.whyItMatters: Multi-plant operations need consistent incident taxonomies and evidence quality across sites.
complianceMatrix.imsControl: Common risk taxonomy, normalized incident report template, cross-site heatmap and closure SLA tracking.
complianceMatrix.evidence: Unified incident register, SLA scorecard, site comparison dashboard

knowledgeQuestions.q: What evidence is needed for a near-miss audit?
knowledgeQuestions.a: The IMS evidence pack should include event time, plant and zone, video frame reference, model confidence, safety threshold breached, supervisor review, CAPA owner, closure SLA and DPDP redaction/access logs.

knowledgeQuestions.q: How does DPDP affect incident reporting?
knowledgeQuestions.a: Incident records may include personal data in video, shift logs or worker identifiers. The demo shows purpose tagging, tokenization, face/identity redaction, role-based access and retention clocks before evidence is exported.

knowledgeQuestions.q: Why does Maruti need cross-site compliance tracking?
knowledgeQuestions.a: When Gurgaon, Manesar and Gujarat use one incident taxonomy, leaders can compare risk patterns, closure quality and audit readiness instead of manually reconciling separate plant reports.

knowledgeQuestions.q: What action should be taken first?
knowledgeQuestions.a: The first action is prevention: alert the floor marshal, slow or reroute the material carrier, and create a high-priority CAPA for lane separation and shift-handover congestion control.

knowledgeQuestions.q: How does this reduce manual reporting?
knowledgeQuestions.a: The Documentation Agent pre-fills the report, attaches evidence, maps compliance obligations, redacts personal data and assigns action owners, reducing draft time from 42 minutes to roughly 6 minutes in the synthetic scenario.

auditTrail.action: Detected proximity event and opened near-miss case
Crossed 70% intervention threshold; suggested andon alert
Drafted incident report and attached video evidence reference
Tokenized worker identifiers and logged purpose tag: EHS incident review
Assigned CAPA owners and published review queue

evidenceObjects.name: Video clip reference
evidenceObjects.description: 20-second window around predicted near miss, with frame-level object metadata and confidence scores.

evidenceObjects.name: Zone risk snapshot
evidenceObjects.description: Aisle congestion, dwell time, PPE visibility and traffic movement score for MNS-LG-04.

evidenceObjects.name: CAPA closure record
evidenceObjects.description: Action owner, due date, evidence attachment and supervisor sign-off history.

evidenceObjects.name: Compliance mapping log
evidenceObjects.description: Mappings to BNVSAP/Bharat NCAP readiness, AIS control lineage and DPDP data controls.

dpdpControls.description: Incident evidence is tagged for EHS incident review and audit readiness only.
Faces, badge IDs and raw worker identifiers are masked/tokenized before report export.
Only EHS, plant leadership and authorized compliance reviewers can view evidence.
Video evidence has a defined retention period with escalation override and deletion queue.
Every view/export is logged into the audit trail for review.
